title: GEOMIND-R: An Audited Database, Structural Descriptor and Forward Model for Radionuclide Uptake in Geopolymers
description:
An audited, provenance-tracked meta-analysis database of caesium and strontium uptake and immobilisation in geopolymers and alkali-activated materials, together with the analysis pipeline and an interactive exploration tool (a local Gradio app plus a self-contained Colab notebook).

The work establishes that framework aluminium (tetrahedral AlIV), not specific surface area, governs cation uptake — evidenced on three independent levels: correlation, a within-sample causal leaching test, and published atomic-scale spectroscopy. It delivers a within-class forward model that predicts held-out samples (leave-one-out R² = 0.81) behind an explicit domain guard.

It further shows that the same descriptor–property relationship does not survive cross-laboratory pooling (leave-one-out R² < 0, worse than predicting the mean) — a general caution for materials-informatics meta-analysis — and contributes reusable screening methods, including a Langmuir saturation screen (θ = 1 − RL) that flags extrapolation artefacts in reported capacities, and a two-pool audit framework that keeps adsorption and immobilisation data separate.

The manuscript reporting these results is withheld pending journal submission and peer review; the data, code and analyses needed to reproduce every number are included here.
